title: NOC 65310 – Light duty cleaners | GTR Immigration
description: Light duty cleaners under NOC 65310 are the essential maintenance professionals who ensure the health, safety, and aesthetic standards of Canada's indoor environments. Operating in hotels, office buildings, hospitals, and private residences, these TEER 5 workers are the backbone of the facilities management industry. In 2026, as Canada continues to prioritize high-sanitation standards in public and private spaces, this role serves as one of the most accessible and high-volume entry points for international workers. For immigration, this occupation is a cornerstone of the Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP) and the BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled stream.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level5Major Group65 – Sales and service support occupationsSub-Major Group653 – Support occupations in personal and specialized servicesMinor Group6531 – Cleaning and environmental support occupationsNOC Code65310

duties: Sweep, mop, wash, wax, and polish floors in offices, hotels, and public buildings.
Dust furniture and vacuum carpeting, area rugs, draperies, and upholstered furniture.
Make beds, change sheets, and distribute clean towels and toiletries in hotels and hospitals.
Stock linen closets and supplies storerooms.
Clean, disinfect, and polish kitchen and bathroom fixtures and appliances.
Pick up debris and empty trash containers.
Report and store lost and found items.
Provide information about the establishment to guests and residents when required.
employment_requirements: No formal education is usually required, though some secondary school is often preferred.
On-the-job training is provided for all specific cleaning protocols and the use of chemical cleaners.
WHMIS Certification: Most employers require or provide training for the Workplace Hazardous Materials Information System (WHMIS).
Reliability and a strong work ethic are highly valued by Canadian employers.
Basic proficiency in English or French is required for safety compliance and coordination.
job_outlook: The outlook for this occupation is Favourable. Projections indicate over 80,000 new job openings nationally due to industry growth and the replacement of workers moving into supervisory roles such as Cleaning Supervisors (NOC 62024). The hospitality sector's post-pandemic expansion continues to drive record-high recruitment for room attendants and hotel cleaners.
labour_market_demand: The demand for light duty cleaners is Very Good and remains consistent year-round. In 2026, the industry is seeing a significant surge in demand in Ontario, British Columbia, and Alberta, particularly within the hospitality and healthcare sectors. With the expansion of new corporate headquarters and high-density residential developments, cleaning companies are actively seeking international talent to fill these essential gaps.
Rapid Employment: High demand and low entry barriers mean you can often secure a job offer quickly after arrival.
Essential Industry Stability: Work in a sector that is vital to the public health and operational efficiency of all Canadian businesses.
Flexible Work Hours: Many cleaning roles offer evening, night, or weekend shifts, allowing for a flexible lifestyle or the pursuit of additional studies.
Clear Path to PR: A high-priority occupation for many "Semi-Skilled" provincial immigration pathways.
pathways: Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion route for workers with job offers from designated employers in the Atlantic provinces.
BC PNP Entry-Level and Semi-Skilled (ELSS): A dedicated pathway for those working in the tourism/hospitality sector for 9 consecutive months in BC.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: Fast-track PR for those currently working in Alberta on a valid work permit in eligible service sectors.
OINP Employer Job Offer (In-Demand Skills): Targeted at specific service roles that support Ontario’s labor market infrastructure.
